title: Living in Boulder vlog 4/17/18: Audio History Books, done exersizing

enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs from Boulder on April 17, 2018, explaining that he has quit exercising for now and discussing his current project: building a free audio history books page on his website. He wants to memorize the contents of important public-domain history books by listening to them repeatedly, and describes his ideal daily routine of combining book listening with bike rides and video creation.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Has quit exercising for now and is okay with that
Believes it is achievable to memorize all important free history books by listening repeatedly every two weeks
Strongly prefers free public-domain books so the collection can be comprehensive without cost barriers
Wants to wake up excited to read/listen to a book, combine it with bike rides, and vlog about it every day
Wants to do it but recognizes it is a massive time sink (a year of work) and is deliberately deferring it

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I love being in Boulderfield Log.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I went on two rides recently.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I had to do three out of your streetbooks on my website before I could ride.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to listen to music while riding but might switch to audiobooks when I get bored.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm working on organizing my free audio history book page on my website.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I like all ancient history books until around the year 600.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to include photos alongside the books to help with memorization.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I have free audio history books available from a little liberal box and Kendall.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The books have to be free because the system is designed to allow random selection from about 2000 books.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I made the page to memorize all the important free books from the past.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Important books from the past are mostly free on Provox or Kendall.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I estimate that memorizing these books is feasible, similar to how people memorize the Bible.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Listening to the books repeatedly every two weeks helps with memorization.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I built the system to create a 'bubble of knowledge' to avoid distractions.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Once memorized, this knowledge can be applied to history tests.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm currently working on adding links and chapters to the UK and US sections of my website.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to record YouTube videos about the books once the website is complete.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to listen to the audiobooks on my MP3 players during commutes.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My goal is to be excited every day about reading and working on books.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to superimpose my bike rides on a map of Rome.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Working on placing Rome on Google Earth could take a year.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Creating daily content about books could attract significant income.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I might delay making videos until I finish my current project.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I measured my height recently and found it to be 13.25, smaller than before.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I got some pizza recently.
